description_long: This cathedral features a grand, elongated nave with a series of prominent flying buttresses supporting the upper walls, characteristic of Gothic architecture. The structure is dominated by two massive square towers at one end and a tall, slender central spire rising above the crossing. The roof is steeply pitched and covered in grayish stone or slate, while the walls and buttresses are constructed from a lighter, beige stone. The apse at the opposite end of the towers is rounded and encircled by radiating chapels, visible through the ribbed supports. Large window openings are present along the nave, though detailed stained glass is not clearly visible. The overall appearance is monumental, with strong vertical lines and a sense of upward movement.
description: A large Gothic cathedral with twin towers, a tall central spire, flying buttresses, and a rounded apse. The structure is built from light stone with a steeply pitched roof and ribbed supports.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the nave, flying buttresses, and the central spire rising above the crossing.
description_view_1: Angled view highlighting the rounded apse, radiating chapels, and the ribbed supports at the rear of the cathedral.
description_view_2: Opposite side view with the nave, buttresses, and the spire visible, as well as the rear apse structure.
description_view_3: View dominated by the two massive square towers and the central spire, with the nave partially obscured.
attribute_facade_ornamentation: The main facade features minimal visible ornamentation, with structural emphasis on the towers and pointed arches. Decorative elements such as statues or tracery are not clearly visible in the images.
attribute_entry_portal_design: The main entry portal is set within the base of the twin towers and appears to have a pointed arch design. Decorative features are subtle, with no prominent sculptures or elaborate carvings visible.
